text.title: Schemas and Dependency Injection
text.summary: Pydantic v2 schema hierarchy, Depends() for DB sessions and auth, and type aliases for cleaner route signatures.

section.title: Schema hierarchy

rule.statement: Define separate Base, Create, Update, and Response schemas. Response schema uses ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) for ORM object serialization. Never reuse one schema for all four roles.
rule.rationale: A single schema cannot express that Create requires password but Response must not expose it, and Update makes all fields optional. Separate schemas make each constraint explicit.

section.reference: Full schema set with pagination response

rule.statement: Use Annotated type aliases (DBSession, CurrentUser) for dependencies. This eliminates repeated Depends() boilerplate and makes route signatures readable.
rule.rationale: Without aliases, every route parameter uses Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)] — verbose and hard to update if the dependency changes.

example.code:
from typing import Annotated from fastapi import Depends from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from app.db.database import async_session from app.models.user import User
async def get_db() -> AsyncSession: async with async_session() as session: try: yield session await session.commit() except Exception: await session.rollback() raise
# Type aliases — use these in route signatures DBSession = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)] CurrentUser = Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_active_user)]
